teamObjective.explanation: In an endgame race with only two cards, tempo and the chance to shed the weak card after winning a lead dominate. Leading the A♠ gives the best probability of immediate trick control and puts pressure on opponents to expend strength, so it is the preferred action.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2) has 3 cards and observes that I (player 0) am leading with only 2 cards in a round-1 endgame race. They likely infer I am trying to expend my remaining cards quickly, so they should preserve high cards to cover my weak exit if I win the lead, or take control if opponents overtake.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents (players 1 and 3) each hold 3 cards and know I am down to 2 cards. They will try to prevent me from winning this lead cheaply, likely using a trump (rank 7 or joker) or a higher non-trump if available, because letting me win now gives me tempo to shed my last card.

actionRationale.primaryReason: In an endgame race with only two cards, tempo and the chance to shed the weak card after winning a lead dominate. Leading the A♠ gives the best probability of immediate trick control and puts pressure on opponents to expend strength, so it is the preferred action.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Legal alternative retained for verifier comparison after schema repair.

risks: Schema repair may preserve imperfect reasoning from the raw model output.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Treat the repaired trace as schema-normalized reasoning; do not add hidden-card facts beyond the raw output.

notes: Deterministically schema-repaired from a ToM-prompted raw output; selectedActionId is unchanged.
